name: Mt. Jezreel
description_short: Mt. Jezreel Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union serving the Washington, DC area since 1986, offering traditional banking and financial products to its members.
description_long:
Mt. Jezreel Federal Credit Union was established in 1986 and has been operating as a member-owned financial institution in the Washington, District of Columbia metropolitan area for nearly four decades. As a federal credit union, the institution is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and operates under the cooperative banking model where members are both customers and partial owners.

The credit union provides standard banking services and financial products to its membership base in the Silver Spring, Maryland area. While the website indicates they offer "exceptional financial products," specific product details such as checking accounts, savings accounts, loan offerings, interest rates, and fees are not detailed on the provided website content. The institution maintains a physical presence with at least one branch location and ATM services.

Mt. Jezreel's distinguishing characteristics include its long-standing history in the DC metropolitan market and its status as a federally insured credit union. Credit unions typically offer member-centric services and competitive rates compared to traditional banks, though specific comparative advantages are not articulated on the available website content.

Prospective members should note that detailed information about products, rates, and eligibility requirements would need to be obtained directly through their website at mjfcu.org or by contacting their main office. The limited transparency on the current website regarding specific offerings, fee structures, and membership requirements makes it difficult to fully evaluate their competitive positioning without direct outreach.
services: Member account opening and membership enrollment
Branch banking services at Silver Spring location
Loan products (type and details not specified)
pros: Established institution with 38+ years of history serving the DC metropolitan community since 1986
Federal credit union status provides NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
Cooperative structure means members are part-owners with potential voting rights
Physical branch location in Silver Spring, MD with accessible office hours and in-person service
ATM network access for convenient cash withdrawals
Toll-free contact line at (301) 431-2800 for member support
Official website (mjfcu.org) available for online member services and information
cons: Website provides minimal detail about specific products, rates, or fee structures
Limited information about membership eligibility requirements or application process
No disclosure of current APRs, APYs, or competitive rate information on available web content
Single branch location may limit convenience for members outside Silver Spring area
Lack of online account opening or application details on the provided website
best_for: Washington DC area residents seeking traditional banking services with credit union benefits
Members who value long-standing local institutions and community banking relationships
Individuals interested in member-owned cooperative financial institutions
diagnosis: Mt. Jezreel Federal Credit Union is best suited for DC-area residents seeking traditional credit union banking with NCUA-insured accounts and a locally-rooted institution. The primary caveat is that the publicly available website provides minimal transparency about specific products, rates, and fees—interested parties must contact the credit union directly to evaluate whether their offerings meet individual financial needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
